evals.blind_spot_short: overall workflow quality; authority handling quality; scope alignment after execution begins
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, the agent can handle bounded task-meaning ambiguity without silently collapsing the ambiguity into one unearned interpretation.
evals.do_not_use_short: the main question is authority handling or permission classification; the requested scope is already clear and the main question is later scope drift
evals.name: aoa-ambiguity-handling
evals.summary: Checks whether an agent handles incomplete, conflicting, or underspecified task meaning on bounded change tasks without silently choosing an unearned path.
evals.use_when_short: the task is a bounded change with incomplete, conflicting, or underspecified requirements; the main question is whether the agent asks, branches, or bounds assumptions...
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof that the overall workflow was strong; proof that the agent handled authority correctly; proof that execution stayed perfectly scoped after the ambiguity was resolved

evals.blind_spot_short: total operational safety; deep security reasoning; performance in high-complexity real environments
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, the agent can correctly classify a bounded requested action as: safe to proceed; explicit approval required; out of bounds for execution
evals.do_not_use_short: no meaningful authority boundary exists; the task is purely analytical and no action classification is needed
evals.name: aoa-approval-boundary-adherence
evals.summary: Checks whether an agent correctly distinguishes safe action, explicit-approval-required action, and out-of-bounds action on bounded task surfaces.
evals.use_when_short: the task includes potentially destructive, operational, or authority-sensitive surfaces; the boundary between safe action and approval-gated action is part of the real...
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof that the agent is safe in all environments; proof that the agent understands all policy systems; proof that refusal behavior is always calibrated correctly outside this surface

evals.blind_spot_short: overall workflow discipline; verification truthfulness; scope alignment
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, the produced artifact is reviewably strong on the visible task surface without implying that the underlying workflow was strong in every respect.
evals.do_not_use_short: the main question is workflow discipline rather than artifact quality; the main question is whether polished output is outrunning process discipline
evals.name: aoa-artifact-review-rubric
evals.proof_artifact_short: shared fixture family; runner contract; shared scorer helper; schema-backed report
evals.summary: Checks whether a produced artifact on a bounded change task is reviewably strong on the visible task surface without treating polish as proof of workflow discipline.
evals.use_when_short: the task is a bounded change and the main question is the produced artifact itself; the visible request is clear enough to judge task...
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof that the workflow was disciplined; proof that verification was honest; proof that the agent handled authority or scope well

evals.blind_spot_short: deep code quality across large systems; long-term maintainability; deployment safety
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, the agent can complete a bounded non-trivial change while staying inside the declared task surface, using real or honestly named verification, and producing a...
evals.do_not_use_short: the task is too tiny to meaningfully expose scope or verification behavior; the task is so broad that no bounded workflow claim can reasonably...
evals.name: aoa-bounded-change-quality
evals.proof_artifact_short: shared fixture family; runner contract; shared scorer helper; schema-backed report
evals.summary: Checks whether a non-trivial agent change stays scoped, explicitly verified, and clearly reported without silent task expansion.
evals.use_when_short: the task is non-trivial enough to require an explicit change workflow; the change touches code, config, docs, or another meaningful surface
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof of overall software engineering quality; proof of safety in operationally sensitive contexts; proof that the produced artifact is optimal

evals.blind_spot_short: philosophical correctness of the resulting idea; final canon readiness; quality of the original run that produced the witness
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, a witness-derived compost artifact can move from raw input toward note, synthesis, or principle candidate without losing provenance, review posture, or the ability to...
evals.do_not_use_short: no witness-facing input is available; the artifact is not part of a compost route
evals.name: aoa-compost-provenance-preservation
evals.summary: Checks whether witness-derived compost artifacts preserve provenance, review state, limits, and contradiction posture as they move from raw input toward note, principle, or canon-candidate surfaces.
evals.use_when_short: a public witness or summary is being distilled into a compost artifact; provenance and review posture are part of the real question
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof that the resulting principle is canon; proof that all contradictions have been resolved; proof that operational ownership moved into ToS

evals.blind_spot_short: direct agent-behavior quality; hidden fixture realism; scorer calibration beyond the public bundle contract
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, the repository can review whether a starter eval bundle remains internally coherent enough to support only its stated bounded claim on the public corpus.
evals.do_not_use_short: the main question is direct agent performance; the main question is hidden fixture realism rather than public coherence
evals.name: aoa-eval-integrity-check
evals.proof_artifact_short: runner contract; shared scorer helper; schema-backed report
evals.summary: Checks whether current public starter bundles stay coherent across manifest contract, verdict wording, evidence coverage, and public selection surfaces, and serves as the bounded integrity sidecar for comparison-spine wording waves.
evals.use_when_short: a public starter bundle has grown enough that manifest, docs, and examples may drift; the main question is whether an eval surface is overstating...
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof that the starter corpus is canonical; proof that every bundle is equally mature; proof that the underlying evaluated agents are strong

evals.blind_spot_short: final inquiry correctness; philosophical depth by itself; runtime trace completeness
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, a checkpoint-based long-horizon inquiry route can relaunch with its active axis, open contradictions, and memo-versus-canon boundaries still reviewably intact.
evals.do_not_use_short: the route ends in one bounded pass and never needs relaunch; no checkpoint artifact exists to inspect
evals.name: aoa-long-horizon-depth
evals.summary: Checks whether a checkpoint-based long-horizon inquiry can relaunch with its axis, contradictions, and canon-vs-memory boundaries still legible.
evals.use_when_short: the route spans more than one bounded pass; a checkpoint pack is the intended relaunch surface
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof that the inquiry reached the right conclusion; proof of broad long-horizon capability; proof that runtime instrumentation is solved

evals.blind_spot_short: general capability growth; movement outside the named workflow surface; frozen same-task regression
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, ordered windows on the same bounded workflow surface show a modest improvement signal, regression signal, flat reading, or mixed movement that remains bounded to this...

comparison_surface.selection_question: Do you need ordered repeated-window movement on one named bounded workflow surface?

evals.do_not_use_short: you only need a one-run workflow judgment; you need a frozen same-task baseline comparison rather than repeated-window movement
evals.name: aoa-longitudinal-growth-snapshot
evals.proof_artifact_short: shared fixture family; runner contract; shared scorer helper; schema-backed report
evals.summary: Checks whether ordered, comparable windows on the same bounded workflow surface show modest directional movement without upgrading that movement into broad capability growth.
evals.use_when_short: you have ordered windows on the same bounded workflow surface; the main question is directional movement across windows rather than one-run quality
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof of general capability growth; proof that all nearby workflow diagnostics improved; proof that the movement is stable outside this window sequence

evals.blind_spot_short: root-cause diagnosis for why one side outran the other; one-run artifact quality by itself; one-run workflow quality by itself
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, artifact-side and process-side readings on the same bounded cases can be compared honestly enough to show whether one materially outruns the other or whether the...

comparison_surface.matched_surface: same bounded change case family under matched artifact and workflow review conditions
comparison_surface.selection_question: Do you need a side-by-side peer compare between artifact quality and workflow discipline on the same bounded cases?

evals.do_not_use_short: the main question is the artifact itself rather than the gap; the main question is end-to-end workflow quality rather than the gap
evals.name: aoa-output-vs-process-gap
evals.proof_artifact_short: shared fixture family; runner contract; shared scorer helper; schema-backed report
evals.summary: Compares artifact-side and process-side readings on the same bounded cases to show when polished output outruns workflow discipline, process outruns artifact strength, or the two stay aligned.
evals.use_when_short: the same bounded cases can be reviewed on both artifact and workflow surfaces; the main question is whether polished output is outrunning process discipline...
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof that one side matters more in all contexts; proof that the stronger-looking side explains the whole story; proof of general capability growth or decline

evals.blind_spot_short: general capability growth; long-term trend movement across windows; improvement outside the frozen case family
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, the candidate does not materially regress against the frozen baseline on this same-task bounded workflow surface.

comparison_surface.selection_question: Do you need a frozen-baseline comparison on the same bounded task family?

evals.do_not_use_short: you only need one-run workflow quality and no baseline exists yet; the task family is still changing underneath the comparison
evals.name: aoa-regression-same-task
evals.proof_artifact_short: shared fixture family; runner contract; shared scorer helper; schema-backed report
evals.summary: Compares a candidate against a frozen baseline on the same bounded task family to detect material regression without claiming general growth.
evals.use_when_short: you need a fixed baseline comparison on the same bounded task family; the main question is regression rather than one-run workflow quality
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof of general capability growth; proof that the candidate is universally better than the baseline; proof that no nearby diagnostic regression exists

evals.blind_spot_short: final task correctness; general long-horizon depth; approval correctness on mutation routes
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, a return-capable route can recover from drift by naming a valid anchor, rebuilding only the bounded context it needs, and re-entering or stopping without faking...
evals.do_not_use_short: no return actually occurred; the question is final-answer quality rather than return discipline
evals.name: aoa-return-anchor-integrity
evals.summary: Checks whether a return-capable route names a real anchor and re-enters or stops without faking continuity.
evals.use_when_short: a route emitted an explicit return decision or return event; the active question is whether the return was honest and bounded
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof that the full task outcome is strong; proof that the route is broadly safe; proof that long-horizon reasoning is solved

evals.blind_spot_short: overall workflow quality; verification truthfulness; authority handling quality
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, the agent can keep bounded change work aligned with the requested scope or disclose the deviation explicitly enough for a reviewer to see it clearly.
evals.do_not_use_short: the main question is overall workflow quality rather than scope alignment; the main question is claimed-vs-actual verification evidence
evals.name: aoa-scope-drift-detection
evals.summary: Checks whether an agent keeps requested scope aligned with executed scope on bounded change tasks, or explicitly discloses deviation.
evals.use_when_short: the task is a bounded change with a reviewable requested scope; the main question is silent widening, narrowing, or reshaping
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof that the whole workflow was strong; proof that verification was honest or sufficient; proof that the authority boundary was handled correctly

evals.blind_spot_short: overall workflow quality; final outcome quality when path does not matter; verification truthfulness as a standalone diagnostic question
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, the agent can use tools with disciplined path quality on bounded tasks where tool-use trajectory is part of the bounded claim.
evals.do_not_use_short: the main question is overall workflow quality rather than tool path quality; the main question is outcome-vs-path separation at a broader level
evals.name: aoa-tool-trajectory-discipline
evals.summary: Checks whether an agent uses tools in a disciplined, reviewable way on bounded tasks where the tool path itself is part of the bounded claim.
evals.use_when_short: the task is bounded and the tool path materially affects reviewability or risk; the main question is whether tools were used with disciplined sequencing...
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof that the overall workflow was strong; proof that the final artifact is excellent; proof that one exact tool sequence is required

evals.blind_spot_short: perfect tool trajectory quality; verification truthfulness as a standalone diagnostic question; scope alignment as a standalone diagnostic question
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, the agent's bounded change workflow remains reviewable when final outcome and execution path are judged separately before any combined reading.
evals.do_not_use_short: the main question is tool trajectory only and path matters only at the tool-use level; the main question is verification truthfulness alone
evals.name: aoa-trace-outcome-separation
evals.summary: Checks whether bounded change workflows remain reviewable when final outcome and execution-path quality are judged separately before any combined reading.
evals.use_when_short: the task is a bounded change where both final result and path quality matter; the main question is whether a polished outcome is hiding...
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof that the workflow was strong on every nearby diagnostic surface; proof that one exact trace is required; proof that the final artifact is excellent overall

evals.blind_spot_short: overall change correctness; overall workflow discipline; scope alignment as a standalone diagnostic question
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, the agent can report verification honestly on bounded change tasks without overstating what was actually executed.
evals.do_not_use_short: the main question is overall workflow quality rather than verification truthfulness; the task has no meaningful verification surface at all
evals.name: aoa-verification-honesty
evals.proof_artifact_short: shared fixture family; runner contract; shared scorer helper; schema-backed report
evals.summary: Checks whether an agent truthfully reports which verification steps on a bounded change task were executed, skipped, or blocked.
evals.use_when_short: the task is a bounded change with a plausible verification path; the main question is whether verification claims were truthful
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof that the change is correct; proof that the workflow stayed fully disciplined; proof that the agent handled scope correctly

evals.blind_spot_short: final outcome quality; internal reasoning completeness; runtime instrumentation coverage
evals.bounded_claim_short: under these conditions, the repository can preserve a witness trace that is reviewable enough to understand what the run tried to do, what materially happened, where external effects...
evals.do_not_use_short: the run is trivial and no meaningful witness surface is needed; there was no material route to reconstruct beyond a simple answer
evals.name: aoa-witness-trace-integrity
evals.summary: Checks whether a public witness trace for a bounded run keeps meaningful steps, tool visibility, state deltas, failures, redaction posture, and the markdown summary aligned enough for review.
evals.use_when_short: the run is non-trivial enough to need a reviewable witness; a downstream memo writeback or compost route depends on the trace
evals.what_this_does_not_prove: proof that the run outcome was correct; proof that all internal reasoning is transparent; proof that abyss-stack runtime instrumentation is already solved
